title: Aspidium rivulorum
intro:
Unfortunately, I do not have information on the plant Aspidium rivulorum, however, I can provide information on Aspidium (which is an old name for the genus Dryopteris), specifically Dryopteris rivulorum (also known as Dryopteris pteridioides), a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae.

Aspidium or Dryopteris is a genus of ferns that are often referred to as "wood ferns." The health benefits of Aspidium or Dryopteris species include their ability to act as a diuretic, which may help to reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections.

The bioactive constituents of Aspidium or Dryopteris species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and lignans, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Aspidium or Dryopteris species include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory tract infections.
taxonomy: Aspidium rivulorum is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a part of the genus Aspidium, which is now often considered to be a synonym for the genus Dryopteris. Therefore, some sources classify Aspidium rivulorum as Dryopteris rivulorum. This classification reflects the ongoing debate and changes in the taxonomy of ferns. Aspidium rivulorum is native to tropical regions, including parts of Africa and Asia.
uses: Aspidium rivulorum, a species of fern, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world.
